payload.goal: Update the helper CLI PRD with install wizard user stories and related requirements.
payload.why: The iteration-1 helper lacks an easy install wizard for macOS/Linux global usage; the PRD should capture this as traceable requirements and user stories.
inputs_already_verified: User decision: zsh PATH updates should go to ~/.zshrc (not ~/.zprofile).
Wizard must include PATH updating and detection of the correct local shell config.
Scope: macOS + Linux; global install intent; minimal friction; transparent prompts with OS-sensitive defaults.
changes: In docs/opencode-helper-cli.md, add functional requirements for installation and PATH configuration (interactive wizard + non-interactive flags).
If appropriate, add/update non-functional requirements for idempotent, deterministic shell config edits and safe defaults (no sudo unless user selects system dir).
Add a new feature entry for an install wizard (e.g., FEAT-009 - Install Wizard) and link it to the new requirements.
Add new user stories for: (1) global install with minimal friction, (2) interactive install wizard with OS-sensitive defaults and PATH updates. Ensure stable IDs (continue numbering after US-011).
Update PRD success criteria/user value bullets if they should explicitly include global install + PATH configuration.
Update the traceability matrix to include the new requirement(s), feature, and user stories.
tests: No code/tests required; ensure internal doc links and IDs remain consistent.
Verify new requirement/user story IDs are unique and referenced correctly.
done_when: docs/opencode-helper-cli.md contains new REQ-* entries covering install wizard + PATH config.
docs/opencode-helper-cli.md contains new FEAT-* entry for install wizard linked to REQ-*.
docs/opencode-helper-cli.md contains 2 new US-* stories with clear acceptance criteria including shell detection (zsh uses ~/.zshrc).
Traceability matrix references are updated and consistent.
abort_if: Adding the new stories/requirements would require changing code behavior semantics beyond documentation scope.
The next available US/REQ/FEAT IDs cannot be determined from the document.
examples: Acceptance criteria should mention idempotent PATH block insertion and non-interactive flags like --yes and --bin-dir.
Shell configs: zsh -> ~/.zshrc; bash -> ~/.bashrc (Linux) or ~/.bash_profile (macOS) with a documented rule; fish -> ~/.config/fish/config.fish.
